title: 10 best herbal teas for insomnia

remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian tea contains compounds like valerenic acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Muscle relaxation properties: The alpha-linolenic acid present in valerian root helps to relax muscles, reducing muscle cramps, spasms, and restlessness, allowing for a more peaceful sleep.

GABA-enhancing properties: Valerian tea contains GABA-like compounds that enhance the activity of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), an inhibitory neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, making it easier to fall into a deeper sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with insomnia because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The roots of the valerian plant contain a group of compounds called valerenic acids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. As a result, valerian teas can help regulate sleep patterns, calm the mind, and induce a restful night's sleep. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate insomnia and improve overall sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene acid that helps reduce anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation and calmer sleep patterns.
Valeranone: A valerenic acid derivative that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
Isovaleric acid: A short-chain fatty acid that has a sedative effect and can help reduce restlessness, promoting a more peaceful and restorative sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative effects.
Stems: While less commonly used than roots and rhizomes, stems are sometimes used in tea blends to provide a milder sedative effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the fall when the plant is mature and the roots are fully developed for better flavor and medicinal properties.
Clean and dry the harvested roots thoroughly for at least two days to prevent any contamination or bacterial growth.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and measure out 2 to 3 teaspoons per 8 ounce cup of water for a standard infusion.
Steep the chopped roots in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain and serve the tea immediately to prevent any further extraction of the active ingredients.

remedy_properties: Calming effects on the nervous system: Lemon balm's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to relax the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to insomnia.

Regulation of sleep-wake cycles: Lemon balm has been found to increase the levels of the neurotransmitter GABA, which helps regulate sleep-wake cycles, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing symptoms of insomnia.
Sedative properties: Lemon balm's essential oils have sedative effects, helping to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with insomnia because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and apigenin that have a relaxing effect on the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. Lemon balm tea also promotes a sense of calmness and tranquility, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to insomnia. Its gentle sedative properties help to regulate sleep patterns, leading to a more restful and rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinatannins: These phenolic compounds help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, contributing to improved sleep quality.
Volatile oils (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): These terpenes have a sedative effect, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calmness that can aid in falling asleep.
Hesperidin: This flavonoid has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety, promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of melissa essential oil, which has a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to induce relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Flowers: As they contain a high concentration of melissa essential oil, they contribute to the sedative properties of the tea, promoting a peaceful and restful sleep.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and melissa essential oil, the buds of Melissa officinalis help to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in late morning when the dew has evaporated but the plant is still cool.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Measure 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried melissa officinalis leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their flavorful oils.
Strain the melissa officinalis tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Passionflower's flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can lead to improved sleep quality.
Apigenin: This polyphenol has sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep.

Volatile oils: Maypop tea contains a variety of volatile oils, including terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the body, promoting relaxation and reducing insomnia.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, teas helps with insomnia because it contains flavonoids and alkaloids that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The herb has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. By decreasing the activity of the nervous system, Passiflora incarnata tea can help individuals relax and fall into a restful sleep. Its sedative properties can also help alleviate stress and promote a sense of calm, leading to a more peaceful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Hernandezioside: This flavonoid glycoside has been shown to possess sedative properties, which may help alleviate insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid is believed to possess a sedative effect by acting on the GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) receptor, a key neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep and wakefulness.

Harman alkaloids: Specifically, harmine and harmaline have been identified as potential contributors to the sedative effects of Passiflora incarnata. Harmine has been shown to inhibit the activity of monoamine oxidase, an enzyme that breaks down neurotransmitters like serotonin, which plays a role in regulating sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that promote relaxation and sedation.
Fruits: The fruits are used as they contain a high concentration of passifloric acid and other antioxidants that help in reducing stress and anxiety.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high amount of alkaloids, including passiflorine, which is known for its sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry a quantity of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves by spreading them out on a paper tray for 3 to 4 days.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Measure out one teaspoon of the powdered herb and place it into a teapot or infuser for serving.
Steep the herb in a cup of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes then strain the liquid.
Serve the passiflora incarnata tea hot or chilled according to individual preference and enjoy immediately.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in hops tea have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep due to their calming effects on the nervous system.
Lupulones: Lupulones are a type of sesquiterpene compound found in hops that have been proven to act as a natural sedative, helping to induce sleepiness and improve sleep quality.
Xanthohumol: Xanthohumol is a flavonoid-based compound in hops tea that has been shown to have a calming effect on the brain, reducing stress and anxiety levels, which can contribute to insomnia.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus, also known as hops, teas helps with insomnia because of its natural sedative properties. The tea contains flavonoids and essential oils that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. Hops also contain a compound called 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep. This natural remedy can be a soothing and effective solution for those struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Humulone: A sesquiterpene, humulone has sedative properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Lupulone: Another sesquiterpene, lupulone has been shown to have a sedative effect and can help reduce sleep latency by promoting a feeling of calmness and relaxation.

Hops flavonoids (specifically xanthohumol and isoxanthohumol): These flavonoids have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to insomnia.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain a significant amount of essential oils, including humulone and lupulone, which have sedative properties that help with insomnia.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the leaves of Humulus lupulus help to relax the mind and body, promoting better sleep.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called humulene, which has a sedative effect and can help to reduce stress and anxiety related to insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh humulus lupulus flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a single layer for 7-10 days to achieve a moisture level of 10.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried humulus lupulus flowers with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds from the flowers.
Strain the tea and let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature for consumption.

remedy_properties: Melatonin-enhancing properties: Broad-leaved lime tea contains compounds that can increase melatonin levels in the body, promoting a restful and relaxing sleep-wake cycle.

Anxiolytic and anti-stress effects: The tea's flavonoids and alkaloids have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and anti-stress properties, helping to calm the mind and body before bedtime.

Muscle relaxation and sedative actions: The tea's saponins and other bioactive compounds can help relax muscles, reduce muscle tension, and induce a calming effect, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, teas helps with insomnia because of its calming and sedative properties. The tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which work together to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The soothing effects of Tilia platyphyllos tea can help quiet the mind and prepare the body for a restful night's sleep. Additionally, its natural sedative properties can also help regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle, leading to improved sleep quality and duration. This makes it a popular natural remedy for insomnia sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid that helps with insomnia by acting as a natural sedative and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Triterpenes: A group of compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to a restful sleep.
Flavonoids: A class of compounds that have a soothing effect on the body, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calmness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Tilia platyphyllos leaves are commonly used to make teas for insomnia due to their calming and sedative properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Tilia platyphyllos are used to make teas for insomnia because they contain compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress.
Buds: Tilia platyphyllos buds are used in teas for insomnia as they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to calm the mind and body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in late spring when the buds are just about to open.
Dry the harvested tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to create tea.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes allowing the flavors and oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve and enjoy hot or cold as desired.

remedy_properties: Baicalin: A flavonoid compound found in skullcap that has been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic effects, helping to calm the mind and body to promote relaxation and reduce stress-related insomnia.
Scutellarein: Another key flavonoid in skullcap that has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which can help to alleviate muscle tension and restlessness, common contributors to insomnia.

Volatile oils: Skullcap tea contains a variety of volatile oils, including borneol, linalool, and beta-caryophyllene, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to induce relaxation and improve sleep quality.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scutellaria lateriflora, also known as skullcap, teas helps with insomnia because it contains flavonoids and other compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The tea's calming effects are attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of stress hormones, such as cortisol, allowing the body to relax and prepare for sleep. As a natural sedative, Scutellaria lateriflora tea helps to quiet the mind, regulate sleep patterns, and improve the overall quality of sleep. This makes it an effective herbal remedy for individuals struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Baclofen-like compounds: These compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, act as GABA receptors agonists, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can lead to better sleep quality.
Baicalein: This flavonoid has sedative and hypnotic properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to insomnia, and promoting a restful night's sleep.

Scutellarin: This flavonoid has been shown to have a sedative effect by inhibiting the activity of certain neurotransmitters that regulate sleep-wake cycles, promoting relaxation and reducing insomnia symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Roots: The roots are often used for their sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help with relaxation and sleep.
Flowers: The flowers are sometimes used for their calming effects, which can aid in reducing stress and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Measure out dried scutellaria lateriflora root in quantities of 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured scutellaria lateriflora root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes or until the liquid reaches your desired strength.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool for 10 minutes before serving it in small amounts as needed.

remedy_properties: Apigenin: An antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. (Why: Because apigenin helps calm the mind and body, creating a conducive environment for sleep.)

Chamazulene: A sesquiterpene compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, further enhancing sleep quality. (Why: Because chamazulene's calming effects on the muscles and nervous system create a sense of calmness, making it easier to fall asleep.)

Aroma: The soothing aroma of chamomile tea has been shown to slow down breathing rates and lower blood pressure, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, ultimately leading to improved sleep quality. (Why: Because the calming scent of chamomile tea signals to the brain that it's time to relax, making it easier to wind down and fall asleep.)
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with insomnia because of its soothing and calming properties. The tea's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, interact with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Chamomile tea has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Its gentle, non-stimulating effects allow for a restful night's sleep, helping to alleviate insomnia and promote a good night's rest.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, helping to increase the activity of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has a sedative effect by inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to insomnia, and promoting a calming effect on the nervous system.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anxiolytic properties, which help reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and improving the quality of sleep.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make teas for insomnia due to their calming and soothing effects, which help promote relaxation.
Seeds: They are used to make teas for insomnia because they contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has sedative properties.
Leaves: They are used to make teas for insomnia as they possess anti-inflammatory and calming properties, contributing to a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried flower heads of matricaria chamomilla to ensure sufficient tea quantity.
Measure 2 teaspoons of the dried flower heads for a standard serving.
Steep the dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve to remove loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) effects: Lavender tea's anxiolytic properties help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety, which are common causes of insomnia.
Sedative effects: The sedative properties of lavender tea can promote relaxation and drowsiness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to insomnia and other sleep disorders.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with insomnia because of its calming and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. As a result, individuals can fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. The tea's calming aroma also helps to calm the mind and body, making it an effective natural remedy for insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural sedative, helping to calm the mind and body, and promoting relaxation and sleep.
Linalyl acetate: Similar to linalool, this terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to insomnia.
Luteolin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, leading to improved sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their calming and soothing properties, which promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Leaves: Lavender leaves have similar properties to the flowers and contain calming compounds that can help reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to fall asleep.
Stems: Lavender stems contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the leaves, which can be used to create a more potent tea that promotes relaxation and eases insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Heat 8 ounces of water to 212 degrees fahrenheit in a medium saucepan.
Pour the hot water over the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in a large mug.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or according to personal preference.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.

remedy_properties: Chamomile: Chamomile contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Lavender: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which helps to quiet the mind and body before sleep.

Valerian root: Valerian root contains valerenic acid, which interacts with GABA receptors, increasing the activity of a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and sedation, helping to induce a restful night's sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with insomnia because of its unique combination of L-theanine and caffeine. L-theanine promotes relaxation by regulating the body's response to stress, while caffeine stimulates the brain without causing a sudden spike in alertness. This balance creates a calming and focused state, ideal for unwinding before sleep. The soothing effects of Camellia sinensis tea also reduce anxiety and promote a peaceful atmosphere, helping individuals fall asleep faster and enjoy a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoid compounds help with insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels due to their ability to inhibit the activity of inflammatory enzymes and oxidative stress.

L-theanine: This amino acid constituent of Camellia sinensis helps with insomnia by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, leading to a calming and relaxing effect.

Catechins (mainly epigallocatechin gallate, EGCG): These polyphenolic compounds help with insomnia by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and promoting a restful sleep due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis are the most commonly used part for teas, and they can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality due to their calming effects.
Buds: The buds of Camellia sinensis are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce stress and anxiety, leading to a better night's sleep.
Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis are the most commonly used part for teas, and they can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality due to their calming effects.
Buds: The buds of Camellia sinensis are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce stress and anxiety, leading to a better night's sleep.
Flowers: The flowers of Camellia sinensis can be used to make a calming tea that helps reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves by hand in early morning when dew is still present for best flavor.
Clean the harvested leaves by rinsing them under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steep one teaspoon of loose-leaf camellia sinensis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot to remove leaves and sediment.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy as is or add sweetener and milk to taste.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key component of licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, common underlying causes of insomnia.
Isoflavones: Isoflavones present in licorice root have been shown to exhibit sedative properties, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in licorice tea have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with insomnia because of its calming and relaxing properties. The roots of the plant contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, promoting a peaceful state of mind. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help individuals achieve a restful night's sleep and improve overall sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help with insomnia by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can improve sleep quality.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, aids in insomnia by reducing stress and anxiety, thereby facilitating a restful sleep.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help alleviate insomnia by acting as a natural sedative, calming the mind and body, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make teas for insomnia due to their soothing and calming properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are also used in teas for insomnia, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in teas for insomnia, as they possess a sweet, licorice-like flavor and aroma, promoting a calming and peaceful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable supplier or grow your own in a well-drained area.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Grind 5 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and enjoy immediately while the flavors are at their peak.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with insomnia because it contains saponins, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. When consumed as a warm tea, these saponins can help to quiet the mind and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, the soothing properties of marshmallow root can help to ease stress and anxiety, common contributors to insomnia. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, herbal marshmallow teas can help individuals drift off to sleep more easily and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_properties: Soothing demulcent properties: Marshmallow tea's soothing and protective coating helps to calm the digestive system, reducing discomfort and anxiety that can contribute to insomnia.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in marshmallow tea may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

Glycans and mucilage content: Marshmallow's unique glycans and mucilage components create a soothing gel-like substance that helps to calm the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort that can disrupt sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Barks: The barks are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce anxiety and promote a good night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root and place it in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Boil fresh water by bringing one cup of water to a rolling boil for exactly three minutes.
Steep the dried marshmallow root in the boiling water for seven to ten minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup and discard the solids.
Allow the cooled tea to steep for an additional five to ten minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry teas helps with insomnia because of its unique blend of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds. These powerful antioxidants help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that often contribute to difficulty falling asleep. The tea's relaxing properties also promote a sense of tranquility, making it easier to drift off to sleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, highbush cranberry teas may help regulate the body's internal clock, ensuring a restful and rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Highbush cranberry tea contains a rich amount of antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, leading to improved sleep quality and duration.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in highbush cranberry tea may help regulate blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, common symptoms of insomnia.

Cyanidin-3-glucoside (C3G): C3G is a powerful anthocyanin found in highbush cranberry tea that has been shown to have sedative effects and may help reduce stress and anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant have a calming effect, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making them ideal for treating insomnia.
Barks: The barks of the highbush cranberry plant contain compounds that can help regulate sleep patterns and promote a good night's rest.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried american cranberry (vaccinium macrocarpon) leaves in 15g quantities for steeping.
Boil filtered water in a kettle to 95°c and pour it over the dried herb mixture.
Allow the tea to steep for 7-10 minutes to release the bioactive compounds effectively.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding any solids remaining.
Drink the warm tea immediately or let it cool to room temperature within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats teas helps with insomnia because they promote relaxation, calmness, and tranquility. The soothing properties of herbs such as lavender, chamomile, and valerian root in oat tea help to quiet a racing mind and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, the calming effects can also help to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, leading to a restful night's sleep and feeling refreshed in the morning.
remedy_properties: Soothing Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Oat teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.

Calming Amino Acids: Oats are rich in calming amino acids like tryptophan and 5-hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP), which are converted into serotonin and melatonin in the brain, helping to regulate sleep patterns and induce a sense of calm.

Sedative Flavonoids: Oat teas contain flavonoids like quercetin, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, oat roots help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Stems: Containing a unique combination of phenolic acids and lignans, oat stems have been shown to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 teaspoons of dried oat straw with boiling water to remove any impurities.
Steep the oat straw in 8 ounces of hot water for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to eliminate solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well to create a soothing and calming infusion.
Drink the tea 30-60 minutes before bedtime to promote relaxation and improved sleep.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with insomnia because of its calming properties, which promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The tea's active ingredients, including triterpenoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, inducing a sense of tranquility and reducing stress levels. As a result, purple coneflower teas can help regulate sleep patterns, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night, leading to a restful and rejuvenating slumber.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Purple Coneflower tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to improved sleep quality.

Sedative and Calming Effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Purple Coneflower tea, including ursolic acid and beta-amyrin, have been shown to possess sedative and calming properties, helping to soothe the mind and body and promote relaxation.

GABA-Enhancing Properties: Purple Coneflower tea contains compounds that have been found to interact with GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid), a neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep and relaxation. This may help increase GABA's calming effects, promoting deeper sleep and improved insomnia relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: Containing a unique blend of fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and induce restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or one teaspoon of loose-leaf tea into a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour it over the echinacea mixture in the cup, allowing it steep for eight minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally as it steeps to ensure even extraction of the herbal compounds.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Drink the warm echinacea purpurea tea immediately and aim to consume one to two cups per day for optimal sleep benefits.

remedy_recipe: Measure 10g of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit into a small bowl.
Heat 200ml of boiling water in a saucepan and pour over the ziziphus jujuba.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary tea has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body that may contribute to insomnia. Boil one cup of water and pour it over the rosemary leaves in a teapot.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried citrus x aurantium peel per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat 8 ounces of pure filtered water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to insomnia that are treated with herbal teas are anxiety, stress, and restlessness. Chamomile tea, for example, is known for its calming effects, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of anxiety and restlessness. Valerian root tea has also been used to treat sleep disorders and anxiety, while passionflower tea helps to calm the mind and body. Lemon balm tea is another natural remedy that can help alleviate insomnia-like symptoms by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for insomnia are natural remedies that help alleviate sleep disorders by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The best herbal teas for insomnia include Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Humulus lupulus, Camellia sinensis, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, and Scutellaria lateriflora. If left untreated, insomnia can severely impact daily life, leading to fatigue, decreased productivity, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with insomnia is a blend of Chamomile, Lavender, and Valerian root. Chamomile calms the mind and soothes the body, while Lavender promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety. Valerian root, a natural sedative, helps regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle. Together, these teas create a soothing and calming effect, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. This trio is a natural and effective way to combat insomnia and promote a restful night's sleep.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Australian family physician, researchers searched the AMED and MEDLINE databases for literature on the efficacy of valerian and hops in treating primary insomnia. The study included 16 primary sources, out of which 12 found that using valerian, alone or in combination with hops, improved sleep parameters such as sleep latency and quality of sleep. This suggests that herbal teas containing valerian and hops may be beneficial for individuals experiencing insomnia, as they can help improve sleep quality and duration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas helps with insomnia because it contains a unique blend of compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The tea's active ingredients, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, interact with the brain's GABA receptors, calming the nervous system and inducing a peaceful sleep. By reducing anxiety and promoting a restful state, Valeriana officinalis teas can help individuals fall asleep faster and enjoy a deeper, more restful sleep, leading to improved overall well-being and a refreshed morning.

Valeranolic acid: Valeranolic acid has been found to possess sedative and anticonvulsant properties, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce the likelihood of insomnia.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried valeriana officinalis root in a bowl for herbal tea preparation.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried root for each 8 oz of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured root with 8 oz of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving hot or at room temperature.

Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the measured melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain and serve the melissa officinalis tea hot or chilled within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried lavender flowers and store them in an airtight container to preserve freshness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the humulus lupulus cones in the morning after the dew has evaporated using scissors or pruning shears.
Rinse the harvested cones in cold water for 5 minutes to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the rinsed cones in a single layer on a paper bag or a tray for 2 weeks.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of the dried cones per 8 ounces of boiling water for a standard infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas helps with insomnia because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds and calming effects. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 fresh camellia sinensis leaves and buds on a dry morning at dawn.
Rinse the leaves with cool water to remove dirt and impurities gently.

Strain the liquid through a metal tea strainer into a teapot or cup carefully.

Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves from the liquid.
